effect.trigger: first mention of dermatomyositis in a section

quotes: every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention
After first mention of "dermatomyositis," use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition.
Do NOT use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads
every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention

rules.intent: Ensure clear, accessible, SEO-friendly abbreviation handling that survives section assembly.
rules.rule_text: After the first mention of "dermatomyositis" (using the pattern `dermatomyositis (DM)`), use "DM" in running copy to avoid repetition. Never use "DM" in headlines, headings, or subheads — always spell out the full word there (clarity, accessibility, and SEO). Because sections are assembled later, [GENERAL] every standalone section must establish `dermatomyositis (DM)` at its own first mention unless the assembly brief states the abbreviation is established upstream.

rules.intent: Maintain inclusive, person-first language for patient audiences.
rules.rule_text: In patient materials, say "someone with dermatomyositis" / "people with dermatomyositis" — not "patients." ("Patients" is acceptable only in HCP materials.) Use "care team" (not "staff") for the network of patients, HCPs, and support systems.

quotes: always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios. Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

rules.rule_text: Spell out zero–nine; use numerals for 10+. Exceptions — always numerals: percentages, ages, ratios. Percentages use the % sign (e.g., 74%).

quotes: avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying

rules.intent: Present clinical data in a supportive, trust-building way.
rules.rule_text: When citing clinical data, avoid negative or fear-based language; use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying. Example pattern: "Participants in the VALOR study taking LISRAYA had nearly 50% more improvement in their DM symptoms after one year compared to placebo."

quotes: Oxford/serial comma always in series of 3+.

rules.rule_text: Always use the Oxford/serial comma in a series of 3 or more.
